reasoning: The latest data from AAA lists the national average for regular gasoline at $3.98 as of March 26, 2026. The resolution criteria requires the national average to exceed $5.016 before April 1, 2026. This would require an increase of over $1.03 in just 5 days. Since the total increase over the entire past month (when the Iran conflict began and oil spiked) was only $1.00, and the previous week's increase was only 10 cents, an overnight spike of $1.00+ on a smoothed national average metric is virtually impossible. I am highly confident that gas prices will not reach a new all-time high by April 1, 2026.
